              have h₆ : (fun t : Eucl 1 => x + t.ofLp 0 • v) = (fun t : Eucl 1 => x + (t : ℝ) • v) := by
                funext t
                simp [Fin.sum_univ_succ, Eucl.prod]
                <;> simp_all [Lp.toLp]
                <;> ring_nf
              rw [h₆]
              exact h₅
            exact this.isPlot
          exact h₂, by simp⟩)
      (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1) =
    @DFunLike.coe (Eucl 1 →ₗ[ℝ] @InternalTangentSpace X (by infer_instance) x) (Eucl 1) (fun x_1 => @InternalTangentSpace X (by infer_instance) x)
      LinearMap.instFunLike
      (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X (by infer_instance) x
        ⟨⟨1, fun t => x + t.ofLp 0 • w⟩, by
          -- TODO get fun_prop to handle this
          have h₂ : IsPlot (fun t : Eucl 1 => x + t.ofLp 0 • w) := by
            have : DSmooth (fun t : Eucl 1 => x + t.ofLp 0 • w) := by
              have h₃ : DSmooth (fun t : Eucl 1 => (t : ℝ)) := (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth
              have h₄ : DSmooth (fun t : Eucl 1 => (t : ℝ) • w) := dsmooth_smul.comp (h₃.prod_mk dsmooth_const)
              have h₅ : DSmooth (fun t : Eucl 1 => x + (t : ℝ) • w) := (dsmooth_add_left x).comp h₄
              have h₆ : (fun t : Eucl 1 => x + t.ofLp 0 • w) = (fun t : Eucl 1 => x + (t : ℝ) • w) := by
                funext t
                simp [Fin.sum_univ_succ, Eucl.prod]
                <;> simp_all [Lp.toLp]
                <;> ring_nf
              rw [h₆]
              exact h₅
            exact this.isPlot
          exact h₂, by simp⟩)
      (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1) := by
    simpa [h] using h
  have h₂ : v = w := by
    sorry
  exact h₂

(try aesop)
          <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try linarith)
          <;>
          (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [add_assoc]) <;>
          (try aesop)
          <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try linarith)
          <;>
          (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [add_assoc]) <;>
          (try aesop)
        exact h₅
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  exact h₂

-- The actual proof would use the injectivity of the map from the vector space to its internal tangent space.
  -- For fine diffeologies, this map is injective because the diffeology is sufficiently rich to distinguish vectors.
  -- However, we don't yet have the necessary lemmas to formalize this.
  sorry

have h₁ : v = w := by
    have h₂ := congr_arg (fun x => @DFunLike.coe (Eucl 1 →ₗ[ℝ] @InternalTangentSpace X _ x) (Eucl 1) (fun x_1 => @InternalTangentSpace X _ x) LinearMap.instFunLike (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X _ x ⟨⟨1, fun t => x + t.ofLp 0 • v⟩, _⟩) (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)) h
    simp_all [Eucl]
    <;>
    (try contradiction) <;>
    (try simp_all [LinearMap]) <;>
    (try norm_num at *) <;>
    (try linarith) <;>
    (try ring_nf at *) <;>
    (try field_simp at *) <;>
    (try nlinarith)
    <;>
    simp_all [AddCommGroup, Module]
    <;>
    aesop
  exact h₁

have h₁ : v = w := by
    sorry
  exact h₁

<;>
  (try simp_all [LinearMap.ext_iff, EuclideanSpace.single_apply, Fin.sum_univ_succ]) <;>
  (try {
    have h₁ := congr_fun h (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)
    simp [EuclideanSpace.single_apply, Fin.sum_univ_succ] at h₁
    <;>
    simp_all [InternalTangentSpace.lof, LinearMap.coe_add, LinearMap.coe_smul,
      LinearMap.coe_unique]
    <;>
    aesop
  }) <;>
  (try {
    exfalso
    <;>
    simp_all [IsFineDiffeology]
    <;>
    aesop
  }) <;>
  (try {
    simp_all [IsFineDiffeology]
    <;>
    aesop
  }) <;>
  (try {
    simp_all [LinearMap.ext_iff, EuclideanSpace.single_apply, Fin.sum_univ_succ]
    <;>
    aesop
  })
